speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: We will before the walls of Rome tomorrow

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Set down our host. My partner in this action,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You must report to th’ Volscian lords how plainly

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have borne this business.

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Only their ends

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: You have respected, stopped your ears against

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: The general suit of Rome, never admitted

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: A private whisper, no, not with such friends

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: That thought them sure of you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: This last old man,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whom with a cracked heart I have sent to Rome,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Loved me above the measure of a father,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Nay, godded me indeed. Their latest refuge

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Was to send him, for whose old love I have—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Though I showed sourly to him—once more offered

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The first conditions, which they did refuse

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And cannot now accept, to grace him only

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That thought he could do more. A very little

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have yielded to. Fresh embassies and suits,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Nor from the state nor private friends, hereafter

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Will I lend ear to.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ha? What shout is this?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Shall I be tempted to infringe my vow

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In the same time ’tis made? I will not.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My wife comes foremost, then the honored mold

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Wherein this trunk was framed, and in her hand

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The grandchild to her blood. But out, affection!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: All bond and privilege of nature, break!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Let it be virtuous to be obstinate.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What is that curtsy worth? Or those doves’ eyes,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Which can make gods forsworn? I melt and am not

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of stronger earth than others.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My mother bows,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As if Olympus to a molehill should

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In supplication nod; and my young boy

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hath an aspect of intercession which

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Great Nature cries “Deny not!” Let the Volsces

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Plow Rome and harrow Italy, I’ll never

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Be such a gosling to obey instinct, but stand

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: As if a man were author of himself,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And knew no other kin.

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: My lord and husband.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: These eyes are not the same I wore in Rome.

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: The sorrow that delivers us thus changed

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: Makes you think so.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Like a dull actor now,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have forgot my part, and I am out,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Even to a full disgrace. Best of my flesh,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Forgive my tyranny, but do not say

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: For that “Forgive our Romans.”

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: O, a kiss

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Long as my exile, sweet as my revenge!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Now, by the jealous queen of heaven, that kiss

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I carried from thee, dear, and my true lip

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Hath virgined it e’er since. You gods! I prate

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And the most noble mother of the world

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Leave unsaluted. Sink, my knee, i’ th’ earth;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of thy deep duty more impression show

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Than that of common sons.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: O, stand up blest,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whilst with no softer cushion than the flint

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I kneel before thee and unproperly

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Show duty, as mistaken all this while

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Between the child and parent.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What’s this?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your knees to me? To your corrected son?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Then let the pebbles on the hungry beach

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Fillip the stars! Then let the mutinous winds

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Strike the proud cedars ’gainst the fiery sun,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Murdering impossibility to make

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What cannot be slight work.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thou art my warrior;

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I holp to frame thee. Do you know this lady?

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The noble sister of Publicola,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The moon of Rome, chaste as the icicle

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That’s curdied by the frost from purest snow

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And hangs on Dian’s temple!—Dear Valeria.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: This is a poor epitome of yours,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Which by th’ interpretation of full time

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: May show like all yourself.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The god of soldiers,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: With the consent of supreme Jove, inform

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thy thoughts with nobleness, that thou mayst prove

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To shame unvulnerable, and stick i’ th’ wars

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Like a great seamark standing every flaw

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And saving those that eye thee.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Your knee, sirrah.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: That’s my brave boy!

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Even he, your wife, this lady, and myself

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Are suitors to you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I beseech you, peace;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Or if you’d ask, remember this before:

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The thing I have forsworn to grant may never

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Be held by you denials. Do not bid me

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Dismiss my soldiers or capitulate

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Again with Rome’s mechanics. Tell me not

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Wherein I seem unnatural; desire not

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: T’ allay my rages and revenges with

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Your colder reasons.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: O, no more, no more!

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: You have said you will not grant us anything;

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: For we have nothing else to ask but that

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Which you deny already. Yet we will ask,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That if you fail in our request, the blame

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: May hang upon your hardness. Therefore hear us.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Aufidius, and you Volsces, mark, for we’ll

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text:
Hear naught from Rome in private.
                 
                He
                 
                sits
                .
               Your request?

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Should we be silent and not speak, our raiment

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And state of bodies would bewray what life

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: We have led since thy exile. Think with thyself

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: How more unfortunate than all living women

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Are we come hither; since that thy sight, which should

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Make our eyes flow with joy, hearts dance with comforts,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Constrains them weep and shake with fear and sorrow,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Making the mother, wife, and child to see

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The son, the husband, and the father tearing

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: His country’s bowels out. And to poor we

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thine enmity’s most capital. Thou barr’st us

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Our prayers to the gods, which is a comfort

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That all but we enjoy. For how can we—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Alas, how can we—for our country pray,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whereto we are bound, together with thy victory,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whereto we are bound? Alack, or we must lose

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The country, our dear nurse, or else thy person,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Our comfort in the country. We must find

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: An evident calamity, though we had

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Our wish, which side should win, for either thou

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Must as a foreign recreant be led

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: With manacles through our streets, or else

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Triumphantly tread on thy country’s ruin

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And bear the palm for having bravely shed

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thy wife and children’s blood. For myself, son,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I purpose not to wait on fortune till

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: These wars determine. If I cannot persuade thee

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Rather to show a noble grace to both parts

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than seek the end of one, thou shalt no sooner

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: March to assault thy country than to tread—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Trust to ’t, thou shalt not—on thy mother’s womb

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That brought thee to this world.

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: Ay, and mine,

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: That brought you forth this boy to keep your name

speaker: VIRGILIA
text: Living to time.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              MARTIUS
text: He shall not tread on me.

speaker:
YOUNG
               
              MARTIUS
text: I’ll run away till I am bigger, but then I’ll fight.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Not of a woman’s tenderness to be

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Requires nor child nor woman’s face to see.—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I have sat too long.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Nay, go not from us thus.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: If it were so, that our request did tend

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To save the Romans, thereby to destroy

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The Volsces whom you serve, you might condemn us

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: As poisonous of your honor. No, our suit

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Is that you reconcile them, while the Volsces

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: May say “This mercy we have showed,” the Romans

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: “This we received,” and each in either side

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Give the all-hail to thee and cry “Be blest

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: For making up this peace!” Thou know’st, great son,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: The end of war’s uncertain, but this certain,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That, if thou conquer Rome, the benefit

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Which thou shalt thereby reap is such a name

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whose repetition will be dogged with curses,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Whose chronicle thus writ: “The man was noble,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But with his last attempt he wiped it out,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Destroyed his country, and his name remains

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To th’ ensuing age abhorred.” Speak to me, son.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thou hast affected the fine strains of honor

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To imitate the graces of the gods,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To tear with thunder the wide cheeks o’ th’ air

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And yet to charge thy sulfur with a bolt

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That should but rive an oak. Why dost not speak?

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Think’st thou it honorable for a noble man

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Still to remember wrongs?—Daughter, speak you.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: He cares not for your weeping.—Speak thou, boy.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Perhaps thy childishness will move him more

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than can our reasons.—There’s no man in the world

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: More bound to ’s mother, yet here he lets me prate

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Like one i’ th’ stocks. Thou hast never in thy life

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Showed thy dear mother any courtesy

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: When she, poor hen, fond of no second brood,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Has clucked thee to the wars and safely home,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Loaden with honor. Say my request’s unjust

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And spurn me back; but if it be not so,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Thou art not honest, and the gods will plague thee

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: That thou restrain’st from me the duty which

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To a mother’s part belongs.—He turns away.—

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Down, ladies! Let us shame him with our knees.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: To his surname Coriolanus ’longs more pride

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than pity to our prayers. Down! An end.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: This is the last. So, we will home to Rome

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And die among our neighbors.—Nay, behold ’s.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: This boy that cannot tell what he would have,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: But kneels and holds up hands for fellowship,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Does reason our petition with more strength

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Than thou hast to deny ’t.—Come, let us go.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: This fellow had a Volscian to his mother,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: His wife is in Corioles, and his child

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: Like him by chance.—Yet give us our dispatch.

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: I am hushed until our city be afire,

speaker: VOLUMNIA
text: And then I’ll speak a little.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: O mother, mother!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What have you done? Behold, the heavens do ope,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: The gods look down, and this unnatural scene

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: They laugh at. O, my mother, mother, O!

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: You have won a happy victory to Rome,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But, for your son—believe it, O, believe it!—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Most dangerously you have with him prevailed,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: If not most mortal to him. But let it come.—

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Aufidius, though I cannot make true wars,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ll frame convenient peace. Now, good Aufidius,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Were you in my stead, would you have heard

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A mother less? Or granted less, Aufidius?

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I was moved withal.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I dare be sworn you were.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And, sir, it is no little thing to make

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Mine eyes to sweat compassion. But, good sir,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: What peace you’ll make advise me. For my part,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ll not to Rome. I’ll back with you; and pray you,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Stand to me in this cause.—O mother!—Wife!

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: I am glad thou hast set thy mercy and thy honor

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: At difference in thee. Out of that I’ll work

speaker: AUFIDIUS
text: Myself a former fortune.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Ay, by and by;

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: But we will drink together, and you shall bear

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A better witness back than words, which we,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: On like conditions, will have countersealed.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Come, enter with us. Ladies, you deserve

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To have a temple built you. All the swords

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In Italy, and her confederate arms,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Could not have made this peace.
